metadata.title: Sanaria PfSPZ Challenge With Pyrimethamine Chemoprophylaxis (PfSPZ-CVac Approach): Phase 1 Trial to Determine Safety and Protective Efficacy of Sanaria PfSPZ Challenge With Concurrent Pyrimethamine Treatment That Inhibits Development of Asexual B...
metadata.text: This combination of injections and drugs may include: * Injections of Sanaria PfSPZ Challenge (live, infectious malaria parasites) into a vein * FDA approved antimalarial drug called chloroquine (CQ) * FDA approved antimalarial drug called pyrimethamine (PYR) * FDA approved antimalarial drug called Malarone * The study will last approximately 3-7 months (depending on which group participants are in).

grouped_hits.title: Epidemiology of Plasmodium falciparum Infections in a Semi-Arid Rural African Setting: Evidence from Reactive Case Detection in Northwestern Kenya.
matched_sentences: In northwestern Kenya, Turkana County has been historically considered unsuitable for stable malaria transmission because of its unfavorable climate and predominantly semi-nomadic population; consequently, it is overlooked during malaria control planning.
Reactive case detection of confirmed malaria cases at six health facilities across central Turkana was conducted from 2018 to 2019.

grouped_hits.title: Malaria survey data and geospatial suitability mapping for understanding spatial and temporal variations of risk across Kenya
matched_sentences: In this study, we examined how malaria and interventions varied across Kenya for 2015 and 2020.
Malaria remains a public health concern in Kenya where children and pregnant women are vulnerable groups.

We analyzed the Kenya Malaria Indicator Survey (N = 10,072) for 2015 and, (N = 11,549) for 2020, and climate data with Fuzzy overlay method to examine how malaria and its interventions relate to environmental conditions required for malaria.

grouped_hits.title: Current Status of Malaria Control and Elimination in Africa: Epidemiology, Diagnosis, Treatment, Progress and Challenges
matched_sentences: African countries have made certain progress in malaria control and elimination, including rapid diagnosis of malaria, promotion of mosquito nets and insecticides, intermittent prophylactic treatment in high-risk groups, artemisinin based combination therapies, and the development of vaccines.

However, many challenges are emerging in the fight against malaria in Africa, such as climate change, poverty, substandard health services and coverage, increased outdoor transmission and the emergence of new vectors, and the growing threat of resistance to antimalarial drugs and insecticides.

grouped_hits.title: Drug-susceptibility and molecular characterization of epidemic and endemic malaria in Kenya
matched_sentences: Malaria in Kenya continues to pose a great threat to health.

grouped_hits.title: Evaluation of Three Artemisinin-based Combinations for the Treatment of Uncomplicated Malaria in Childreen in Burkina Faso (CHIMIO2)
matched_sentences: Bakground In Burkina Faso, since the adoption of this new malaria treatment policy in 2005, several studies evaluating the efficacy and tolerance of ACTs have been carried out by different research teams at different sites according to an irregular chronology and according to different methods.

The herein study aims to assess the efficacy and safety of AL, DHA-PPQ and As-Pyr in the treatment of uncomplicated malaria in children in three health districts in Burkina Faso, namely the health districts of Banfora, Nanoro and de Gourcy.

With the introduction of seasonal malaria chemoprevention (SMC) by the Sulfadoxine-Pyriméthamime/amodiaquine combination in almost all health districts of Burkina Faso, ASAQ is no longer recommended for the treatment of uncomplicated malaria in the areas covered by this intervention.

grouped_hits.title: Use of Rapid Diagnostic Tests for Malaria Case Management in Kenya
matched_sentences: The purpose of this study is to investigate the impact of rapid diagnostic tests (RDTs) in the context of a newly implemented malaria case management guidelines using artemisinin-based combination therapy on the malaria prescribing practices of health care workers in Kenya.

To evaluate the impact of RDTs introduced in the context of newly implemented malaria case management guidelines using ACTs compared to new malaria case management guidelines using ACTs and clinical diagnosis on malaria diagnosis and prescribing practices by HCWs caring for patients \>5 years of age.

grouped_hits.title: Health Systems Implementation and Molecular Surveillance of Multiple First-Line Treatments for Uncomplicated Malaria in Western Kenya
matched_sentences: This implementation study evaluated the health systems feasibility, economic costs, and stakeholder acceptability of deploying multiple first-line therapies (MFTs) that were of the artemisinin-based combination therapies (ACTs) type, for uncomplicated malaria in Western Kenya.

COMPARISON COUNTY - MIGORI: \- Continued artemether-lumefantrine (AL) monotherapy throughout study period (n=32,835 patients treated) PARTICIPANTS: Adults and children ≥5 years diagnosed with uncomplicated Plasmodium falciparum malaria at participating health facilities.

Complexity of infection (COI) determined by molecular methods METHODS: * Sample collection: Dried blood spots (n=310) from malaria-positive patients at four timepoints * Molecular analysis: Targeted amplicon deep sequencing using Oxford Nanopore Technology * Health systems assessment: Semi-structured questionnaires, key informant interviews, exit interviews, cost analysis using activity-based costing * Statistical analysis: Chi-square tests, Cochran-Armitage trend tests, machine learning models for resistance prediction SETTING: Government and faith-based health facilities in Homa Bay County facilities received MFTs and Migori County (control) where AL was the only ACT used in Western Kenya, a malaria-endemic region with high transmission intensity.

grouped_hits.title: Plasmodium Vivax Efficacy Trial in Cruzeiro do Sul, Acre, Brazil
matched_sentences: Results from this drug efficacy study will be used to assist the Brazilian Ministry of Health in assessing their national malaria treatment policy for P.

Most countries in the Americas have adopted the WHO Global Strategy for Malaria Control, which relies on prompt and effective antimalarial treatment as the major means of reducing malaria morbidity and mortality (WHO 2008).

Although a wide variety of methods have been used to assess resistance to antimalarial drugs in vivo methods, in vitro drug sensitivity testing, and molecular analyses, most national malaria control programs rely on data from in vivo efficacy trials to assess the efficacy of the current first- and second-line drugs and to decide if charges in malaria treatment policy are needed.

grouped_hits.title: L9LS MAb in Malian Adults
matched_sentences: The purpose of this study is to evaluate the safety and tolerability of a one time SC administration of L9LS in healthy adults in Mali, as well as its protective efficacy against naturally occurring Plasmodium falciparum (Pf) infection over a 6-month malaria season.

grouped_hits.title: Sanaria PfSPZ Challenge With Pyrimethamine Chemoprophylaxis (PfSPZ-CVac Approach): Phase 1 Trial to Determine Safety and Protective Efficacy of Sanaria PfSPZ Challenge With Concurrent Pyrimethamine Treatment That Inhibits Development of Asexual B...
matched_sentences: This combination of injections and drugs may include: * Injections of Sanaria PfSPZ Challenge (live, infectious malaria parasites) into a vein * FDA approved antimalarial drug called chloroquine (CQ) * FDA approved antimalarial drug called pyrimethamine (PYR) * FDA approved antimalarial drug called Malarone * The study will last approximately 3-7 months (depending on which group participants are in).
